title: The Children of the Flood
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A teenager aboard a generation ship discovers the mission is a lie and Earth is still alive.
long_description:
In the year 2154, the generation ship Promise of Morning hurtles toward what its passengers believe is Proxima Centauri. Seventeen-year-old Nova, a maintenance technician born aboard the ship, discovers that the mission telemetry is a fabrication. The ship is not on course for the stars at all — it has been traveling in a closed loop for years, and the Earth they supposedly abandoned in ecological collapse is still broadcasting radio signals.

With the help of the ship's ancient AI, the Archivist, Nova uncovers a conspiracy that reaches back to before launch. Captain Ishida and the command crew kept the ten thousand passengers in cryo-sleep not to protect them, but to hide the truth: the colony plan had no viable destination, and they feared the consequences of admitting failure.

As Nova fights to wake the sleeping thousands and reveal the truth, she must navigate a ship fractured by fear, a captain who believes her lies are mercy, and the impossible question of what happens when a generation ship discovers it has nowhere to go but home.
main_characters: Nova — a seventeen-year-old maintenance technician born aboard the ship
Captain Ishida — the commander of the Promise of Morning, keeper of the lie
The Archivist — the ship's ancient AI, keeper of all data and secrets
Elena Vasquez — Nova's mother, a passenger awakened from cryo-sleep
Duarte — the chief engineer, who helps Nova broadcast the truth
Lieutenant Commander Reyes — an engineer who has known the truth for years
Marcus — a civil engineer who helps form the passenger council
Commander Amara Osei — captain of the Earth rescue vessel Resolve
